product_updates.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
product_updates.content: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

product_updates.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
product_updates.content: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

product_updates.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
product_updates.content: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

product_updates.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
product_updates.content: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

product_updates.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
product_updates.content: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

product_updates.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
product_updates.content: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500; it's the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

product_updates.title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
product_updates.content: Alexa for Shopping offers a voice- and touch-enabled shopping experience across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show smart displays. Alexa for Shopping provides more personalized recommendations and automates the shopping experience across Amazon and other online retailers.

product_updates.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
product_updates.content: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

product_updates.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
product_updates.content: A survey compiled from fintech firm Ramp’s clients’ expense data shows 34.4% of participating businesses are paying for Anthropic services, more than any other AI lab, while only 32.3% pay for OpenAI.

product_updates.title: WhatsApp adds an incognito mode in Meta AI chats
product_updates.content: Meta said these incognito conversations are not saved, and messages will disappear by default once you close the chat.

technical_breakthroughs.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
technical_breakthroughs.content: People report that their personal contact info was surfaced by Google AI—and there’s apparently no easy way to prevent it. A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a month, he said, his phone had been inundated by calls from “strangers” who were “looking for a lawyer,…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: making drugs in orbit and NASA’s nuclear-powered spacecraft
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: A plan to make drugs in orbit is going commercial
technical_breakthroughs.content: Varda Space Industries, a startup that’s been pitching its ability to perform drug experiments in space, says it has signed up the pharmaceutical company United Therapeutics in what may be remembered as a notable step toward in-orbit manufacturing. The idea of building things in outer space for u…
